rag_rules: When repairing AGREE contracts, ensure that all visible identifiers are properly declared and accessible in the current scope.
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type contracts.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex when defining AGREE contracts.
Validate that all identifiers referenced in AGREE clauses are resolved before applying repairs.
Ensure range assumptions and guarantees are placed in component type scopes with concrete bounds or named constants.
Avoid introducing new architecture elements during repair; modify only existing contract clauses.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees must reference valid expressions with known types; use helper declarations or visible features only.
Component type contracts should not include subcomponent port references unless properly represented via variables.
anti_patterns: Do not add new property sets or packages to resolve identifier errors; instead, trace and fix root causes.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes; keep them in implementation scopes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
